reviews.review_msg: A seminar format is Dr. Angus' strength; has high expectations of his students, and welcomes independent, interdisciplinary contributions to the discussions. Students cannot 'slough' through this class. Strongly recommended for anyone considering SFU's Liberal Studies Program.

"Contemporary Chinese Literature" was all about learning to read in historical context and with human sensitivity. Prof. Kong clearly cares about her students and is all about making us better readers. This class would be an excellent choice for westerners wanting to learn to appreciate a large minority culture in Greater Vancouver.

He knows the material for the course (Citizenship and Social Justice) extremely well. If you ever have a question, he'll give you an essay of an answer! Very helpful and willing to spend the extra time to make sure all students understand what he is talking about. His marking is fair. If you put the effort into the course then you will do well.

10-100 pages of reading HW each lecture. Some readings are verbose and hard to grasp (I had to write them in my own words) but I found it super interesting. I'd have hated it with any other prof, but Dr Dutton was super nice, friendly, and graded fairly. I envy how precisely he articulates his thoughts. Def taking future classes with him.
